Aegyo Theater: Korean Television: Come get a glimpse of Korean culture all while watching some of the funniest, nerdiest and most clever TV you will see! We will have some of South Korea's most popular TV shows for your perusal and even provide a quick reference sheet of commonly used Korean words. Come join the fun!
Apprentice Movie Night: A line-up of family-friendly movies for our Apprentices to enjoy!
Best at Drinking: World Tour: Bring a flashlight! Best at Drinking returns to ask the all important question, “Who is the best drinker at Play On Con?” This time contestants will take their drinking skills abroad and travel the world in a musical hooch quest of EPCOT proportions. Big news for non-drinkers! An alcohol free quest is available this year!
Build Your Own Bloody Mary: All the spices and fixin's you could ask for! Come make your own Bloody Mary with or without advice from your host Casey.
Cabaret 101: Join in on some cabaret dance basics with a holiday twist! Dance shoes or socks encouraged, but barefoot is fine, too!
Cantina Counter Service: We'll be serving so you can have some refreshments while enjoying the other events!
Coffee Service: Your morning pick-me-up. Coffee with a variety of creamer and sweetener options will be available. A keurig is available if you'd like to bring your own special pods. 21+ may come inside Mayfield if you'd like some additions to your coffee.
Color Wars!:
Get ready for drama, competition, and a dash of musical mystery in Color Wars: Phantom Edition! Inspired by classic camp Color Wars and infused with a Phantom of the Opera twist, this spirited showdown pits teams against each other in fun, creative challenges—all in pursuit of the coveted POC Marks. Choose your side, embrace the theatrics, and let the games begin… just watch out for the Phantom in the wings!

Teams can even sign up as the event starts, so don't feel like you can't join in last minute.
Con In Review: We consider this convention to be a community event. Your input is not only desired but needed if we are to make POC into the best convention it can be. Please join us as we discuss Play On Con 2026 and plan for POC 20 in 2027.
Costume Contest:
Come one, come all, to the biggest costume gala of the weekend. Awards go to "Best in Show", "Most Creative", and "Fan Favorite". Prizes will also be awarded for other categories that will be announced at the event ("Best Group," "Best Anime Character," etc.). Be there in your best and strut your stuff for this on Saturday night!

All costumes entered in any POC Programming contest must be PG-13 or lower. Family show, folks! Please save the grown-up costumes for the 21+ party area.
Craft & Chill: Bring your supplies for an hour of leisurely group crafting (knitting, crochet, cross stitch, spinning, etc) and with a side of coloring. This is a grown-up oriented event where you can chill and meet other crafting enthusiasts. If you're into coloring, we even have some supplies you can borrow!
Dinner Theater: Quiz Bowl: Are you smarter than a middle schooler? Up to four teams of five can compete using middle school quiz bowl questions from NAQT. Don't be fooled, they'll be plenty difficult and adults should be on the teams. Some audience participation, sit on the side of the team you support!
Holiday TV Episodes: We love a good musical episode in our favorite TV shows! Come see some of our favorites and belt out some tunes if they are yours, too!
How To: Tie Dye: Tie Dye is a great way to make custom clothing without breaking the bank. Learn how to turn fabric into a testament to love and peace! This event includes our Apprentices. BYOT, but a few shirts will be available at the event.
Iron Bartender: Superior Mixmasters battle neophyte challengers in number one challenge to the death! Judges demand drinks with secret ingredient, and bartenders do as they are told. Watch as Cocktail Stadium becomes mighty arena of conflict!
Jam Session: Join in an open jam in the Guthrie Lodge. We have a wide variety of instruments that might be stopping in, including ukuleles, guitars, flutes, and even a hurdy gurdy! Be sure to bring some water. All skill levels are welcome to jam out some tunes!
Kickoff Pool Party: It's a pooltime paradise to start off the weekend! Come jump in the pool and enjoy good tunes!
Malevolent Karaoke: Make your loved ones and friends sing songs they hate for fun and fabulous prizes! Two-person teams are required, and you'll score higher if your partner really knows which songs make you squirm. We'll pair up any single players, though, so feel free to come alone! Judges will assign scores in Malevolence, Performance, and Audience Reaction.
Music: Professor Shyguy: Come join Professor Shyguy for a flurry of chiptune rock. With his anthems to everything from The Guild to Portal, this show is destined to appeal to all your nerdy bits.
Music: Sparkling Noise: Sparkling Noise returns to the stage to bring some of their POC Rock Band tournament songs to life, but this time with real instruments! Jon, Art, Kat, Kate, Logan, Corey, Buehler, and bonus guests come armed with old favorites, new parodies, nerdy rock classics, and a few surprises up their sleeves.
Open Cantina: Come chill out in Lower Mayfield with us! We'll be serving and have the area open for people to relax. There may be classes going on as well.
POC: Holiday Variety Show: A special showcase of the incredible vocal and performance talents of our con staff and casted attendees. This is not your average talent show—this is a vibrant, casted production that shines a spotlight on the voices, stories, and creativity of the people within our community. From powerful solos to dynamic group numbers, you'll experience a range of musical styles, theatrical moments, and pure joy on stage. Whether you're cast in the show, cheering from the audience or clapping along to the beat, this is a show you won't want to miss!
Poolside Karaoke: Croon poolside for some afternoon delight… full karaoke goodness. Sing loud, sing proud, and make everyone at the 4-H know you're there!
Presidential Nominations & Table Flippin:
Let your rage be known! Let out a primal yawp and flip the table -- greatest distance wins. Bonus prizes may be awarded for style, grace, or pure rage.

Presidential Nominations will take place at the beginning of this event.
Princess Story Time: Join Elsa and others for stories, sing-alongs, crafts, and a young apprentice meet-and-greet!
Rock Band Contest: It's BACK! The event that we used to use for all our convention promotion comes out of the mothballs for this year's musical festivities. But remember, all of it counts! So come dressed to the nines and put on a show. You won't just get graded on your skill… your costumes and show will matter as well!
Rocky Horror Picture Show: The Rocky Horror Picture Show with audience partici...pation! Costumes and props are encouraged, though we'll restrict anything that's messy (so no food or anything that can't be cleaned up within a couple of minutes). We'll have a quick 5-10 minute intro at the beginning, then show the movie.
Spooky Stories: Come enjoy some spine tingling and brain bending stories with your host, Hil. She will tell you classic and modern horror tales only the strongest and bravest can withstand. All are welcome, but please note these stories are not appropriate for children or sensitive souls, though Hil will do her best to avoid especially triggering subjects.
Star Wars Trivia (Holiday Special & More: Come join host Tony Black (aka Ohio Tony) for Star Wars (Holiday Special and more) Trivia! Compete in teams or individually to win a fabulous prize. Plus, there will be inflatable lightsabers! Be warned, this will not be easy.
Tis the Season to Make Punch: Nothing says holiday gathering quite like a punch bowl. Join Lisa for a spirited tour through punch's 400-year history, from East India Company sailors to George Washington's Christmas bowl to your next holiday party. Learn the legendary five-element formula, explore many classics, and walk away with recipes that will make you the most dangerous host of the season.
Um, Actually: "Um, Actually" is the game where geeks correct other geeks. The host will read several fandom facts aloud, with each fact having an incorrect piece of information. The player who can best correct the statement must buzz in and make the correction, but don't forget to precede your answer with "Um, Actually."
Welcome Wagon: New to Play On Con? We've all been there! Come join a panel of Play On Con regulars as they share their advice for making the most of the weekend and answer your questions about what to expect.
White Elephant Cocktail: Join your host Jared for White Elephant or Dirty Santa (depending on who you ask). Come choose a cocktail card, but be careful, you might not get to keep it for long! He will jam out the cocktails as they are picked. No gifts please, just bring yourself!
Archery: Whether you imagine yourself as Robin Hood or Hawkeye, you owe yourself a chance to see how you stack up!
Beer Croquet: Join The Reverend for a round of BYOB croquet! One hand MUST be distracted with a beverage to play. Seersucker suits are appropriate. 21+ Only!
Boat On!: Take a leisurely trip afloat on Lay Lake. Pick from Canoes, Kayaks and Paddleboards! Canoeing sessions are limited to 30 people.
Climbing Wall: The day never begins until you cling precariously to a cliff face. This activity should be considered a metaphor.
Cooler Yacht-Zee: We have two sets of large outdoor dice and have a drinking game of Yahtzee (or 2) with them! We will bring a cooler of drinks and you can also BYOB. Ideally right after Beer Croquet.
Nature Run/Walk: Start your day with a morning run or a leisurely walk. Runners can stick to the roads while the hikers hit the trails! What will we find? No bears, plz.
Open Gaming:
With dozens of open tables and our library of hundreds of unique board and card games, you can fill your weekend with as much gaming as you could ever want! Open Gaming will be open from 10am till 2am each day of the convention, starting at 4pm on Thursday and ending at 2pm on Sunday.

Features include a massive space with late night open board and card gaming, stacks of games available for check-out, friendly library staff who love teaching new games, opportunities to learn brand new releases as well as old classics, and “Play to Win” games so you can build your collection.

Don't know anyone? New to boardgaming? No problem! Ask our library team what they recommend!
Artemis: Bridge Simulator:
Ever dreamed of being a crew member on the Enterprise, fighting against a relentless horde of enemy ships to save the galaxy?

The Artemis Spaceship Bridge Simulator brings that experience to life. It is a multiplayer, cooperative, role-based game that allows players to command their very own starship. The odds are stacked against you, of course, so the crew must work together to manage weapons, shields, engines, power systems, and strategic information.

Each session of Artemis lasts approximately 45 minutes and accommodates a crew of six players. Each player will choose a completely unique role: Captain, Navigation, Tactical, Engineering, Science, or Communications.

IMPORTANT: Each Artemis mission begins on the hour. Pick up a ticket for your desired crew position and time-slot at the Open Gaming station, and then report to the Artemis Bridge at the assigned time.
Blood on the Clocktower:
A scream echoes through the sleepy town of Ravenswood Bluff. The townsfolk rush to investigate and find the beloved local storyteller has been murdered, their body hanging limp from the clocktower. As blood drips onto the cobblestones below, a realization slowly dawns… a demon has been unleashed, killing by night and taking on human form by day. Can good find the demon in time? Or will evil overrun this once peaceful town?

Blood on the Clocktower is a game of murder and mystery, lies and logic, deduction and deception for five-to-twenty courageous players and one devious storyteller.

A social game played in person, seated in a circle, each player receives either a ‘good’ or ‘evil’ token with their unique character on it. Good players share information to solve the mystery whilst evil players lie about who they are and what they know.
Board Game Tournaments:
POC 2026 will feature a variety of Board Game Tournaments and "Learn to Play" events!

Thursday: Magical Athletes
Friday: Looot!
Saturday: Castle Combo

New to any of our tournament games? No problem! Each of the tournaments has a "Learn to Play" session at the start of each tournament.
Carnival of Doom TTRPG: Come have a fun day at the carnival and play some games and win some prizes you may or may not know you want or need—they are to die for! We promise you'll have a killer good time! The best part is you never have to leave (even if you want to)! Ages 12+.
Goblin Quest TTRPG: Goblin Quest is a tabletop roleplaying game about slapstick violence, fatal ineptitude, and the greatest adventure of your life. Play five goblins each (in sequence, not parallel) and watch them meet hilarious ends while failing to achieve the most basic tasks. Will they survive the dangerous world of the Great Battle Camp and avoid the attentions of brutal orcs, murderous bugbears, mean-spirited hobgoblins and scary wizards? Probably not. But you'll have fun finding out! Ages 12+.
Johann Sebastian Joust: Johann Sebastian Joust is a local multiplayer no-graphics contact sport video game without video. The objective is to be the last player standing, having protected their controller from being jostled. Competitors eliminate each other by causing a rapid motion in their opponent's controller. Up to 7 players hold PS Move motion controllers, which they must protect from other players. When the music (J.S. Bach's Brandenburg Concertos) plays slowly, the controllers are very sensitive to movement. When the music speeds up, the threshold becomes less strict, giving the players a small window to dash at their opponents. If your controller is ever moved beyond the allowable threshold, you're out!
Just Dance!: Start your morning with song and dance! A hearty round of energetic expression… on a dance pad. Friday's session will be for everyone, and Saturday will host an Apprentice session! Touching grass is overrated!
Royal Blood TTRPG:
You've got old blood in your veins. Sorcerer-King. Witch-Queen. Royal Blood is a tabletop roleplaying game about heists, tarot cards, magic, fate and desperation. It uses a deck of tarot cards to build characters, plan a heist and determine opposition, and to resolve every risky action the players make in an attempt to claim power. Players take on the roles of Royals, desperate modern-day magicians with wizard blood left over from the time of sorcerer-kings and witch-queens.

The Arcane were once human just like the player characters, but vast power and long centuries have twisted them weird and iconic. If you want to steal the power from a demigod you'll have to be clever about it. You can't go in all guns blazing: Royal Blood deals in heists. Ages 12+.
Times Past TTRPG: You wake up in the barracks just like every day (right?). You meet the eyes of your companions and say your good mornings as you always do (right?), but this time as you do you catch a flash of a memory of a feeling of fear, betrayal, confusion, and war. It ends almost before it begins, but it's still there in the back of your mind. You expect this day to be just like "every other day"… except this morning a man runs in panicking and says "The village is under attack by monsters!" What happens next is up to you. Interested? Come play and find out where the group will take the story next! Ages 12+.
Video Games:
Our consoles will be available for free play for most of the weekend, so come in and get your game on! Whether you enjoy the newest fighting games or want to kick it old-school, we've got something for you!

Scheduled Tournaments include: Nidhogg, Super Smash Brothers Ultimate, and DJ Hero. We will also be featuring several "Score Attack" tournaments that you can compete in over the weekend. Play your round, register your score, and you can win. Check the Schedule for more information!
